plan_items: Mutable default argument `defaults={}` in `load_config` — classic Python trap where the dict persists across calls; check if mutation risk exists or if this is safe as read-only.
Unclosed file handle: `open(path)` is passed directly to `json.load` with no `with` statement, risking file descriptor leaks.
No error handling for `FileNotFoundError`, `PermissionError`, or `json.JSONDecodeError` — check whether callers are expected to handle these or if the function should guard/document them.
No validation of the `path` argument (e.g. empty string, None) before passing to `open`.
In-place mutation of `data` dict to merge defaults — verify this is intentional and does not surprise callers who may share or cache the returned dict.
